seo.title: Building Dockerfile best practices and optimization with...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Dockerfile best practices and optimization with Docker and Docker Compose.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to building secure, optimized Docker workflows for web applications. Focuses on practical implementation patterns for development, security, and CI/CD integration.
prerequisites: Basic web application with a Dockerfile

steps.title: Project structure normalization
steps.explanation: Create consistent directory layout with .dockerignore, Dockerfile, and build context. Place Dockerfile in root directory and exclude unnecessary files.
pitfalls: Including source control files in build context
Missing .dockerignore leading to large image sizes

steps.title: Dockerfile optimization
steps.explanation: Use multi-stage builds and minimize layers. Combine installation and cleanup steps in single RUN commands.

code.content:
FROM golang:1.21 as builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN go mod download && go build -o /myapp

FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12
COPY --from=builder /myapp /myapp
CMD ["/myapp"]

steps.title: Security baseline implementation
steps.explanation: Add vulnerability scanning and minimal base images. Verify image integrity with notary.

code.content:
trivy image myapp:latest
notary -s https://notary.example.com verify myapp:latest

steps.title: Docker Compose development setup
steps.explanation: Configure services with build contexts, environment variables, and volume mounts for hot reload.

code.content:
version: '3.8'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - .:/app
    environment:
      - DEBUG=1

steps.title: CI/CD pipeline integration
steps.explanation: Configure build pipelines with cache mounts and image tagging strategies.

code.content:
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    - name: Build Docker image
      run: |
        docker build --cache-from myapp:latest -t myapp:latest .
        docker tag myapp:latest myregistry.com/myapp:$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)

pitfalls: Not invalidating caches on dependency changes

content.conclusion: This implementation provides a secure, optimized Docker workflow. Validate each step with your specific application requirements and security policies.
